questions.text: Carbon forms bonds with other atoms by:
questions.explanation: Carbon is tetravalent and achieves stability by sharing its valence electrons, forming covalent bonds.

questions.text: The property of self-linking among identical atoms to form long chains is called:
questions.explanation: Catenation is unique to carbon because of its small size and strong C-C bond energy.

questions.text: Which allotrope of carbon is used as a lubricant and in pencil leads?
questions.explanation: Graphite has a layered structure with weak van der Waals forces between layers, making it slippery.

questions.text: The first member of the alkyne homologous series is:
questions.explanation: Alkynes contain a triple bond. The simplest stable alkyne is Ethyne (C2H2).

questions.text: Saturated hydrocarbons generally burn with a ________ flame.
questions.explanation: Saturated hydrocarbons undergo complete combustion in sufficient oxygen, producing a non-sooty blue flame.

questions.text: Which functional group is present in CH3CH2OH?
questions.explanation: The -OH group indicates an alcohol (Ethanol).

questions.text: Butanone is a four-carbon compound with the functional group:
questions.explanation: The suffix '-one' denotes a ketone group.

questions.text: Vegetable oils are converted into vegetable ghee using ________ gas.
questions.explanation: This is an addition reaction called hydrogenation, usually in the presence of a Nickel catalyst.

questions.text: Ethanoic acid is commonly called:
questions.explanation: Acetic acid is the main component of vinegar.

questions.text: The reaction of an ester with an alkali to give back alcohol and sodium salt of carboxylic acid is:
questions.explanation: Saponification is the process used in the making of soap.

questions.text: Soaps are sodium or potassium salts of long chain:
questions.explanation: Soaps are fatty acid salts.

questions.text: Detergents are generally effective in both soft and hard water because:
options: They do not form insoluble scums with Calcium and Magnesium ions
questions.explanation: Unlike soaps, detergents maintain their cleansing properties in hard water.

questions.text: A spherical aggregate of soap molecules in water is called a:
questions.explanation: Soap molecules form micelles where the hydrophobic tails point inwards towards the oil/dirt.

questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the same homologous series as Methane?
questions.explanation: Methane and Propane are both alkanes following the CnH2n+2 formula.

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures are called:
questions.explanation: For example, Butane and Iso-butane are structural isomers.

questions.text: The next higher homologue of C2H4 is:
questions.explanation: C2H4 is Ethene (alkene). The next member adds a -CH2- unit, becoming Propene (C3H6).

questions.text: Which catalyst is used in the addition reaction of ethene to ethane?
questions.explanation: Palladium or Nickel are commonly used catalysts for hydrogenation.

questions.text: What is the general formula for Alkanes?
questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with single bonds.

questions.text: What happens when Ethanol reacts with Sodium metal?
questions.explanation: 2Na + 2CH3CH2OH -> 2CH3CH2ONa + H2.

questions.text: 5-8% solution of acetic acid in water is called:
questions.explanation: Vinegar is widely used as a preservative in pickles.

questions.text: The hydrophilic end of a soap molecule is:
questions.explanation: The ionic part (COONa) is water-loving (hydrophilic).

questions.text: Diamond is a non-conductor of electricity because:
questions.explanation: In diamond, every valence electron is involved in a strong covalent bond.

questions.text: Which gas is evolved when Ethanoic acid reacts with Sodium Carbonate?
questions.explanation: Acids react with carbonates to produce CO2 gas.

questions.text: Oxidation of ethanol using alkaline KMnO4 produces:
questions.explanation: Alkaline KMnO4 is a strong oxidizing agent that converts alcohols to acids.

questions.text: The general formula for Alkenes is:
questions.explanation: Alkenes contain at least one double bond between carbon atoms.

questions.text: How many single covalent bonds are present in a molecule of Pentane (C5H12)?
questions.explanation: There are 4 C-C bonds and 12 C-H bonds, totaling 16.

questions.text: Which of the following represents the aldehyde functional group?
questions.explanation: -CHO is the aldehyde group, appearing at the end of a carbon chain.

questions.text: Unsaturated hydrocarbons add hydrogen in the presence of catalysts to become saturated. This is a/an ________ reaction.
questions.explanation: The pi bond breaks to allow new atoms to be added to the molecule.

questions.text: Buckminsterfullerene is an allotrope of carbon with ________ atoms.
questions.explanation: It has a shape like a football and contains 60 carbon atoms (C60).

questions.text: Why does carbon not form C4+ ions?
options: It requires too much energy to remove 4 electrons
questions.explanation: Removing four electrons from a small carbon atom requires a massive amount of energy.

questions.text: Pure ethanoic acid has a melting point of 290K and freezes in winter, hence it is called:
questions.explanation: It looks like ice in its solid form, giving it this name.

questions.text: Substitution reactions are characteristic of:
questions.explanation: In the presence of sunlight, Chlorine replaces Hydrogen atoms in alkanes.

questions.text: The difference in molecular mass between two consecutive members of a homologous series is:
questions.explanation: The members differ by a -CH2- unit (12 + 2*1 = 14).

questions.text: Which of the following is used to test the presence of unsaturated hydrocarbons?
questions.explanation: Unsaturated compounds decolorize red-brown bromine water.

questions.text: Esterification involves the reaction between an acid and a/an ________.
questions.explanation: Acid + Alcohol -> Ester + Water (in presence of acid catalyst).

questions.text: Esters are characterized by their ________ smell.
questions.explanation: Because of their pleasant smell, esters are used in perfumes and flavouring agents.

questions.text: Denatured alcohol is ethanol mixed with poisonous substances like ________ to prevent misuse.
questions.explanation: Methanol makes the ethanol unfit for human consumption.

questions.text: Hard water contains salts of:
questions.explanation: These ions react with soap to form insoluble scum.

questions.text: The hydrophobic end of soap is ________ in water.
questions.explanation: The hydrocarbon chain avoids water but dissolves in oil/grease.

questions.text: How many electrons are shared in a double covalent bond?
questions.explanation: Two pairs of electrons (total 4) are shared between the two atoms.
